preamble: The shift from the postalveolar fricative [ʃ] to the retroflex stop [ʈ] is a form of fortition (hardening) mediated by retroflexion. In languages with established retroflex series, the postalveolar sibilant can be retracted slightly to become a retroflex fricative [ʂ], which then hardens into a complete stop.

examples.note: Historical retroflex fricatives frequently correspond to retroflex stops in daughter languages or loanword adaptations.
In complex clusters, the retroflexion of the sibilant can trigger the hardening of following dental elements into a single retroflex stop.
